body:
## Summary

Minimal runner-stability follow-up split out from PR #21.

This keeps only the two changes we want to land now:

- Ignore generated agent outputs in the canonical repo and installation guidance:
  - `.agent/node_modules/`
  - `.agent/dist/`
- Harden `dispatchWorkflow(...)` so a PR-branch runtime can dispatch a default-branch workflow whose `workflow_dispatch` input schema has not caught up yet. If GitHub rejects specific unexpected input names, the helper retries once without only those rejected keys.

Intentionally excluded from this PR:

- no `checkout-pr` force-checkout / generated-dist cleanup changes
- no rubrics-review timeout change

The timeout/default-timeout work should land in a separate PR with a more systematic library/runtime design.

## Validation

- `npm --prefix .agent ci`
- `npm --prefix .agent test` — passed (`376/376`)
- `git diff --check`
- workflow/action/template YAML parsed with Ruby `YAML.load_file`
- shell syntax checks for installed agent scripts/actions

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The dispatch retry stays in the existing GitHub helper module, reusing `commandErrorText(...)` and preserving existing `dispatchWorkflow(...)` call sites. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to ignore rules, install docs/skill guidance, one helper, and one focused test file. PR body explicitly excludes unrelated checkout and timeout work. |
| workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Unsupported-input dispatch failures are not swallowed; the helper retries only when it can remove rejected keys and logs `console.warn(...)` before retrying. |
| workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | No parent issue or linked discussion was present in the live PR body/comments; the PR description documents intended scope and exclusions. |
| workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Installation guidance was updated in `.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md` and `.skills/install-agent/SKILL.md` for `.agent/dist/` and `.agent/node_modules/`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | The docs add concise generated-output ignore guidance, not a per-file inventory table. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The retry logic is a small parser plus one retry path; it avoids workflow-level branching or broader runtime redesign. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric fit is surgical scope: PR #36 contains only the two requested behavior changes and avoids the explicitly excluded cleanup/timeout work.
- The dispatch compatibility fix has focused regression coverage in `.agent/src/__tests__/github.test.ts`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #36 is a narrow runner-stability follow-up split out from PR #21.
- It updates generated-output ignore guidance for `.agent/dist/` and `.agent/node_modules/` so built/runtime artifacts do not leak into repository changes.
- It also hardens `dispatchWorkflow(...)` so workflow dispatch retries once without inputs that GitHub reports as unsupported by the live default-branch workflow schema.
- The PR intentionally excludes checkout cleanup and timeout/default-timeout work for separate follow-up.

## Review
The combined reviews support shipping this PR. The only noted concern is an acceptable INFO-level dependency on GitHub’s current error text format, with safe fallback behavior and focused regression coverage.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues | INFO | Reviewers found no required branch-change work. The dispatch retry is narrow, documented by tests, and preserves original failure behavior when the compatibility path does not apply. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review already passed with no violations.
- The PR already includes docs updates for install guidance and generated-output ignores.
- Focused regression coverage was added for unsupported workflow dispatch inputs.
- Existing PR conversation and inline comments contained no unresolved duplicate feedback; no new inline comments were posted.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: There is no unresolved actionable branch-change work for automation to perform.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #36.

no rubric changes

PR #36 had no trusted human review comments or inline discussions. Its durable lessons were already covered by existing active rubrics: surgical changes, simple implementation, reuse existing code, keep docs in sync, read linked context, and surface stateful failures. Bot review/synthesis comments were advisory only, so I did not learn new user/team preferences from them.

title: Fix dispatch compatibility and generated output ignores
